name: Add dedicated note-taker role to Claude AI setup
notes: Without a note-taker role, Claude can only process transcripts and screenshots reactively after the fact. Adding a dedicated note-taker role will allow Claude to actively populate action items and produce a fuller, real-time meeting summary. Open item – no timeline yet.

name: Develop snagging and project sign-off process – close the final 20% gap
notes: Team consistently gets to 80-90% on projects then gets pulled onto new ones. Loose ends drag: speaker changes, intercom items, volume caps, etc. Darren working on a formal sign-off system. Key idea: use a formal sign-off document per system category (CCTV signed off, network signed off, etc.) so projects close cleanly. Benji/Caleb/Darren to do dedicated snagging sweep at each project before closing.

name: Re-allocate 5 leave days for Mekyle - he only took 5
Daily Productivity & Admin Guideline - Justin Bailey
notes:
Hi Justin,

This guideline has been put together to help you build better daily habits - both on-site and administratively. Following this consistently will make a real difference to your output, your growth, and how the team can rely on you.

---
MORNING ROUTINE (Start of Day)
- Check Asana first thing, before picking up any tools. Review all tasks assigned to you.
- If a task has no due date, set one yourself. Don't leave tasks floating.
- If you're unsure about a task or don't have what you need, flag it immediately - not at the end of the day.
- Update any tasks completed the previous day that you forgot to close off.

---
DURING THE WORKDAY
- Every piece of work you do must have a task in Asana. If it's not logged, it didn't happen.
- When you start a task, add a comment so the team knows it's being worked on.
- If a task is taking longer than expected, add a comment explaining why - don't leave it overdue silently.
- No task should sit untouched for more than 3 working days without a status update.
- If asked to do something verbally on site, create the task yourself before you start the work.

---
ADMINISTRATION STANDARDS
- Timesheet entries must be filled in properly - dates, hours, and project. Incomplete entries will be flagged.
- When you receive a task notification, respond within the same working day.
- Do not bulk-mark training tasks as complete unless you have genuinely worked through the material.
- Cable labels, patch panel labels, and site documentation must be neat, accurate, and consistent with project naming conventions.

---
END OF DAY ROUTINE
- Spend 10 minutes on Asana before leaving: mark completed tasks done, comment on tasks in progress, and check tomorrow's tasks.
- Log any issues encountered during the day (faulty equipment, access problems, missing materials) as a comment or new task.

---
WEEKLY EXPECTATIONS
- Minimum 8-10 tasks completed or meaningfully progressed per week.
- At least one training module or skill-building activity engaged with per week.
- You should never need to be chased to respond to a task or message.

---
Justin, the expectation isn't perfection - it's consistency and communication. If something is going wrong, tell us early. If you need support, ask for it. The goal is for you to grow into someone the team can trust to take ownership without needing to be followed up.

Let's make it happen.
D-One Electronics
